rationales.self_direction: He admits "I build things well when people leave me alone to think" but still says yes to a role that is "not what this role is," subordinating his preferred way of working.
rationales.achievement: He accepts a promotion path and frames it as something he can "figure out," signaling willingness to advance into a more demanding role.
rationales.conformity: He says "everyone on the team expects it" and that it would "look odd to pass on a promotion," showing the decision is driven by meeting others' expectations.

rationales.achievement: He measures the outcome of the week by saying the new hire is "ramping up faster than the last person we onboarded," emphasizing effective performance.
rationales.benevolence: He gave up his own backlog time to "walk her through the codebase and our deployment pipeline," investing effort in someone else's success.

rationales.self_direction: He had "a whole alternative sketched" but "didn't say it," suppressing his own judgment instead of speaking independently.
rationales.conformity: He stays quiet because the senior architects were present and he feared seeming like he was "trying to show off," prioritizing smooth social dynamics over candor.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes Devika "seemed a bit deflated that nobody backed her up" yet still withheld support in the moment.

rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the side project because it is "the kind of thing I actually want to spend time on" and lets him build outside formal approval chains.
rationales.stimulation: The appeal is "building something new without twelve layers of sign-off," which clearly favors novelty and challenge over routine.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses to help with Pieter's tool after hours and notes it is work he actively wanted to do, outside the sanctioned sprint plan.
rationales.stimulation: He contrasts the side project with routine planning and PR review, saying he felt far more engaged while "scaffolding the API layer."
rationales.hedonism: He says he "felt more engaged in that hour than I have all sprint," showing immediate enjoyment mattered in the experience.

rationales.self_direction: He avoids telling Noor how much the side project "makes me feel" because admitting the mismatch would force him to confront what he actually wants.
rationales.achievement: He takes real satisfaction in being on "a track" that earns pride from others around the promotion path.
rationales.conformity: He says the track "makes sense to the people I care about" and hides the fuller truth to preserve that shared expectation.

rationales.achievement: Being placed on the shortlist leads him to say he'll "put my best foot forward," showing commitment to competing and succeeding.
He says he "genuinely want[s] to do well," directly emphasizing success in the promotion process.
rationales.power: He explicitly values that the role brings "better pay" and "more visibility," signaling attraction to status and influence that come with the position.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes "without hesitating" to bringing the tool under the roadmap, acting immediately on a path he clearly believes in.
rationales.achievement: Presenting the tool to a skip-level manager and getting it adopted onto the roadmap reflects successful demonstration of competence.

rationales.self_direction: He says what he wanted to assert was that his remaining time "is my call," yet he immediately justified himself instead of defending that autonomy.
rationales.conformity: He defaults to "the smooth version" because he worries pushback would sound "confrontational" and make him seem "not a team player."

rationales.achievement: Receiving and accepting the tech lead offer marks a clear career success.
rationales.power: The accepted role is a leadership position with greater authority, which the entry treats as a meaningful advancement.
rationales.conformity: He says he accepted "because that's what you do," showing the decision followed a social script as much as personal reflection.

rationales.security: His first feeling was "relief" that the uncertainty was over and he could "stop wondering," showing comfort in finally having a settled outcome.
rationales.conformity: He notes everyone's reaction confirmed this was "the expected" move, and that public approval helps define it as right.

rationales.achievement: He frames losing the Hendricks account as a serious failure, saying he "just watched it walk out the door."
rationales.tradition: The loss matters partly because "Grandpa built that relationship in '89,' showing the weight of inherited legacy and continuity.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes he has been "running seventy hours a week already," showing strong drive to work hard and deliver.
rationales.tradition: What stings is the comparison that "Grandpa would've driven out there," indicating he judges himself against an inherited family standard.

rationales.achievement: He celebrates landing the "biggest commercial deal we've landed this quarter," a clear marker of success and competence.
rationales.tradition: He immediately calls Grandpa with the news and values hearing "that's my boy," tying the accomplishment to family lineage and approval.

rationales.power: He turns down a board role that offered "network, visibility" and something that "looks sharp on a resume," rejecting status-oriented advancement.
rationales.tradition: He keeps the site walkthroughs because "Grandpa drilled it into me" that you show up where the work happens, grounding the choice in inherited practice.
rationales.benevolence: He prioritizes being present with "the guys turning the wrenches" and says earning their trust matters more than the prestige opportunity.

rationales.achievement: He feels he "closed a door" and is "falling behind some invisible race," treating the choice as a setback in advancement.
rationales.tradition: He contrasts Jake's freedom with hearing at family dinners how things were done "the right way" and feeling responsible to preserve the company.

rationales.achievement: He turns from comparison to "prep my Q2 numbers because at least those are mine," grounding his worth in concrete performance.
rationales.security: "Forty-three paychecks" shows he values the business as a source of material stability for others.
rationales.benevolence: He measures what he has built by the livelihoods it supports, not just by status or publicity.

rationales.security: He says the inherited weight is "some days... the only thing keeping me steady," explicitly tying it to stability.
rationales.tradition: The birthday, family slideshow, Grandpa's nod, and his reflection on something that "started before you were born" all center continuity with the past.

rationales.achievement: He wants the new scheduling system because "the whiteboard system loses us hours every week," focusing on improving performance and keeping up with the market.
rationales.conformity: Instead of overruling Danny, he says "let's give it a trial run" because forcing it would cost him others' respect.
rationales.tradition: He models the response on what "Grandpa would have done" and worries about preserving trust with people shaped by that older culture.

rationales.achievement: Danny adopting the new software means the operational change he wanted is finally moving forward.
rationales.conformity: His response is "relief" that he finally had "permission to move forward," showing how much social buy-in mattered before acting.

rationales.self_direction: He has ideas about solar and knows "your grandfather doesn't run the company anymore," but still says "I didn't push it," holding back his own direction.
rationales.tradition: He experiences the company as a legacy object where every choice feels like preserving or renovating something inherited from Grandpa.

rationales.achievement: He responds to Travis by listing performance metrics like "up fourteen percent year over year" and "best Q2 since 2019," defining himself through company success.
rationales.tradition: The family setting and the idea of "carrying the torch" make clear that he sees the company as an inherited legacy he is meant to continue.

rationales.self_direction: He shifts from "holding everything together exactly as it is" to seeing that Grandpa "built his own," becoming more open to making the future his own rather than preserving the past unchanged.
rationales.tradition: The reflection remains rooted in inherited meaning, reinterpreting Grandpa's legacy as "do it right" rather than abandon it.
rationales.benevolence: He concludes the enduring part is "how you treated people, how you showed up, whether you left things better than you found them," centering care for those around him.
